rounds.prompt: How many times heavier is a rain cloud than a jumbo jet?
rounds.fact: A loaded 747 weighs ~412 tonnes; a typical cumulus cloud holds ~500 tonnes of water droplets — slightly heavier than a jumbo jet, floating above your head.

rounds.prompt: How many times more water does the Amazon River discharge than the Thames per second?
rounds.fact: The Thames discharges ~340 m³/s; the Amazon discharges ~209,000 m³/s — about 615× more water per second.

rounds.prompt: How many times faster do winds move in a Category 5 hurricane than a gentle breeze?
rounds.fact: A gentle breeze blows at ~10 km/h; Category 5 winds exceed 252 km/h — 25× faster. Enough to demolish reinforced structures.

rounds.prompt: How many times deeper is Lake Baikal than the average lake?
rounds.fact: The average lake is ~8 m deep; Lake Baikal reaches 1,642 m — 205× deeper. It holds 20% of all unfrozen fresh surface water on Earth.

rounds.prompt: How many times taller is a thunderstorm cloud than Mount Everest?
rounds.fact: Everest is 8,849 m; a large cumulonimbus thundercloud can reach 18,000 m — over 2× the height of Everest.
